metadata.title: Whimsey Tarot - Golden Dawn Study
metadata.description: Study the Golden Dawn system through tarot, astrology, and planetary correspondences

decanPage.subtitle: Learn Your Lordly Title by Astrological Sign
decanPage.description: The twelve zodiac signs are grouped into four elements, each corresponding to a tarot suit. Each sign contains three decans, represented by the numbered cards of their respective suit.

courtPage.subtitle: Court Cards
courtPage.description: The sixteen court cards represent the elemental aspects of each suit as embodied in human archetypes. Each is both Fire, Water, Air, or Earth of their suit's element — a specific facet of a greater elemental whole.
courtPage.elementOfElement: As {rankEl} of {suitEl}, this figure embodies the {rankElLower} quality applied to the {suitElLower} realm —
courtPage.fireRankDescription: the active, projective will driving the suit's force forward with authority and initiative.
courtPage.waterRankDescription: the receptive, feeling dimension giving the suit's force depth, empathy, and internal resonance.
courtPage.airRankDescription: the thinking, communicative aspect carrying the suit's force outward through ideas and swift movement.
courtPage.earthRankDescription: the grounding, material aspect holding the suit's force in latent potential, ready to be cultivated into form.

signsPage.subtitle: Major Arcana by Zodiac Sign
signsPage.description: Each of the twelve zodiac signs is attributed a Major Arcana card in the Golden Dawn system. These trump cards embody the archetypal qualities of their corresponding signs.

planetsPage.title: The Chaldean Planetary Order
planetsPage.subtitle: From the Sphere of the Fixed Stars to the Sublunary Realm
planetsPage.quote: "As above, so below; as within, so without."
planetsPage.quoteSubtext: The Seven Sacred Wanderers in their celestial dance
planetsPage.clickPrompt: Click on any planet to explore its detailed Golden Dawn correspondences
planetsPage.noSignsRuled: No signs ruled in traditional system
